sakuraStandCodes.title: Sakura Stand Codes
sakuraStandCodes.subtitle: Claim Sakura Stand codes for free rewards such as Tokens, Cash, EXP boosts, and event update bonuses.
sakuraStandCodes.intro: Sakura Stand codes are usually released around new updates, shutdown fixes, milestone events, and content drops. The current game page highlights new codes as part of the latest update cycle, alongside new Specs, new skins, and bug fixes.

steps.detail: Launch the official Sakura Stand experience from the Roblox game page.
Press M or use the menu button, then open the Settings tab.
Paste the code into the Enter Code box and press Enter to claim the reward.
Codes can fail when they expire, require a specific Mastery level, are typed with the wrong capitalization, or were already redeemed on the account.

sakuraStandTrelloDiscord.title: Sakura Stand Trello, Discord, and Official Links
sakuraStandTrelloDiscord.subtitle: Use the official Sakura Stand links to follow updates, check game systems, and reach the live experience.
sakuraStandTrelloDiscord.intro: The official Trello is the main reference hub for Sakura Stand mechanics, while Discord is where players track announcements, patch notes, sneak peeks, codes, and community discussions. The Roblox game page and Sakura Society community page are the safest starting points for the live experience and creator information.

links.label: Official Sakura Stand Trello
links.content: Covers Stands, Specs, moves, skins, items, bosses, quests, events, NPCs, locations, and general game information.
links.use_case: Best for checking ability details, obtain methods, move lists, and game systems.

links.label: Official Sakura Stand Discord
links.content: Used for announcements, update notes, sneak peeks, codes, questions, trading discussions, and pinned community information.
links.use_case: Best for live updates, current code drops, trade checks, and balance changes.

links.label: Sakura Stand Roblox Game Page
links.content: The live Roblox experience page for Sakura Stand by Sakura Society, currently titled around the Naoya and Choso update.
links.use_case: Best for launching the game, reading the official description, checking update notes, and confirming mobile support.

links.label: Sakura Society Roblox Community
links.content: The Roblox community connected to Sakura Stand and its developer Sakura Society.
links.use_case: Best for finding the creator hub behind the experience and confirming the official Roblox-side identity.

sakuraStandBeginnerGuide.title: Sakura Stand Beginner Guide
sakuraStandBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the basic loop, where to check moves, how EXP works, and what new players should do first.
sakuraStandBeginnerGuide.intro: Sakura Stand is a Roblox fighting and farming experience inspired mainly by JoJo's Bizarre Adventure and other anime series. The game supports mobile players, includes Stands and Specs as core build choices, and gives Roblox Premium users a 1.15x XP gain bonus.

steps.title: Read the game warning first
steps.description: Sakura Stand includes full-bright flashing light effects and other visual effects that can affect players with migraine, altered vision, convulsion, or similar sensitivity.
steps.tip: Adjust your graphics and avoid long sessions if flashing effects bother you.

steps.title: Understand the core gameplay loop
steps.description: The game follows a fighting and farming simulator loop: fight enemies, earn progress, improve your character, and work toward stronger Stands, Specs, skins, and update content.
steps.tip: Do not chase every new Spec immediately; first learn one moveset well enough to farm safely.

steps.title: Choose between Stands and Specs
steps.description: Stands and Specs are the main combat identity choices in Sakura Stand. The official Trello is the best place to check moves, obtain methods, and mechanic details before committing resources.
steps.tip: Use Trello search for the exact Stand or Spec name instead of scrolling every column.

steps.title: Use codes early
steps.description: Codes can provide free rewards such as Tokens, Cash, EXP boosts, or update bonuses. Open the menu, go to Settings, enter a working code, and press Enter.
steps.tip: Redeem EXP boosts when you are ready to actively farm, not when you are still learning where the buttons are.

steps.title: Farm XP and build consistency
steps.description: The Roblox game page confirms mobile support and a 1.15x XP gain for Premium users. Progression is smoother when you repeat reliable fights instead of jumping into late-game objectives too early.
steps.tip: Stable farming beats random trading and boss-chasing at the start.

steps.title: Track updates through official channels
steps.description: The current update notes mention new Spec content, new skins, new codes, and bug fixes. Discord and Trello are the key places to follow changes after each update.
steps.tip: Check update notes before assuming an old guide still applies, because game balance changes often.

sakuraStandTierList.title: Sakura Stand Tier List
sakuraStandTierList.subtitle: Compare strong combat picks, trading-value categories, and update-sensitive rankings for Sakura Stand.
sakuraStandTierList.intro: Sakura Stand rankings shift when new Specs, skins, boss content, and balance updates arrive. Use this module as a practical tier overview, then check official Discord pins and Trello cards for the newest move details, obtain methods, and trade-value changes.

tiers.notes: High-value picks commonly tracked for strong combat performance, rare obtain paths, or strong update-era demand.
Strong choices for combat, farming, bossing, or trading interest, depending on the current patch and player goals.
Useful picks with solid movesets or progression value, especially when matched with the right playstyle.
Viable picks that may need more matchup knowledge, better execution, or specific goals to feel worth using.
Lower-priority choices for long-term power or trading, but still useful for learning basic mechanics.
Trading value should be checked through Discord pins and the trading community because demand changes often.

sakuraStandStandsSpecs.title: Sakura Stand Stands and Specs
sakuraStandStandsSpecs.subtitle: Browse the main Stand and Spec routes, from Arrow pulls and classic JoJo evolutions to quest-heavy anime abilities like Naoya, Choso, Sukuna, Roland, and Sandevistan.
sakuraStandStandsSpecs.intro: Sakura Stand builds around two ability paths: Stands, which usually come from Arrows and Stand evolution items, and Specs, which usually come from manuals, masks, bosses, NPC quests, events, or special progression chains. Use this section to decide whether you want a fast starter ability, a PvP-focused Spec, or a long grind route with boss and item requirements.

groups.summary: Fast early-game rolls and classic Stand evolutions. These are the first routes most new players test before moving into longer quests.

entries.name: Star Platinum
entries.obtain: Use a Stand Arrow on Standless.
entries.best_for: New players who want a simple Stand route before farming rarer evolutions.

entries.name: Crazy Diamond
entries.obtain: Use a Stand Arrow on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players who want a straightforward Arrow-pool ability.

entries.name: Golden Experience
entries.obtain: Use a Stand Arrow on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players planning to move into Gold Experience Requiem.

entries.name: White Snake
entries.obtain: Use a Stand Arrow on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players planning the C-Moon and Made in Heaven route.

entries.name: Emperor
entries.obtain: Use a Stand Arrow on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players who prefer distance control instead of pure melee.

groups.summary: Item-based Stand upgrades that require a specific base Stand before the evolution item or location condition matters.

entries.name: The World
entries.obtain: Use Dio's Bone on Unawakened Dio.
entries.best_for: Players who want a classic JoJo progression route.

entries.name: Gold Experience Requiem
entries.obtain: Use a Requiem Arrow on Golden Experience.
entries.best_for: Players looking for a stronger end route from Golden Experience.

entries.name: C-Moon
entries.obtain: Use Green Baby on White Snake.
entries.best_for: Players progressing toward Made in Heaven.

entries.name: Made in Heaven
entries.obtain: Bring C-Moon and Dio's Diary to the Highest Peak during a real-life New Moon.
entries.best_for: Players who enjoy timed condition grinds.

entries.name: Anubis
entries.obtain: Use Haunted Sword on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players who want sword pressure and item-based progression.

groups.summary: Simple Spec routes built around direct item use or map interaction. These are clean training routes before the larger anime questlines.

entries.name: Standless
entries.obtain: Start the game without an ability or use Rokakaka Fruit to reset your current Stand or Spec.
entries.best_for: Starting most item, NPC, and quest routes.

entries.name: Hamon
entries.obtain: Use Hamon Manual on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players who want a simple manual-based Spec.

entries.name: Vampirism
entries.obtain: Use Stone Mask on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players who like direct item unlocks.

entries.name: Spin
entries.obtain: Use Spin Manual on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players who want mid-range pressure from a classic Spec.

groups.summary: High-demand routes centered on cursed items, boss fights, NPCs, and the newer Naoya and Choso update content.

entries.name: Itadori Yuji
entries.obtain: Reach Mastery 1, then follow the Jujutsu progression route tied to cursed enemies and Sukuna's Finger progress.
entries.best_for: Players planning to unlock Gojo or Sukuna later.

entries.name: Gojo
entries.obtain: Complete Gojo's quest while using Itadori Yuji.
entries.best_for: Players who want a popular PvP-focused JJK ability.

entries.name: Sukuna
entries.obtain: Eat 15 Sukuna Fingers with Itadori Yuji, then clear the Sukuna boss route.
entries.best_for: Players who want regeneration, heavy burst, and domain-style pressure.

entries.name: Naoya Zen'in
entries.obtain: Start through Naobito at the abandoned shrine without a JJK Spec equipped, then complete the Zen'in progression and Naoya raid route.
entries.best_for: Players who want a fast, aggressive ability from the Naoya and Choso update.

entries.name: Choso
entries.obtain: Use Death Painting on Standless after farming the Kamo Remnant route.
entries.best_for: Players who want stun pressure, blood shots, and awakening tools.

groups.summary: Longer routes with mastery gates, NPC questlines, boss drops, or multi-step item requirements.

entries.name: Deku
entries.obtain: Progress One For All through its stages, defeat the Deku boss for One For All's Grace, then use All Might's Hair after the final quest step.
entries.best_for: Players who like repeated boss progression and quest chains.

entries.name: Sandevistan
entries.obtain: Reach Mastery 1 with Standless, collect Cyan Cyberdeck, Crimson Cyberdeck, Cortex Support, and Tech Monitor, then complete N4Animation's quest.
entries.best_for: Players who want a Cyberpunk-inspired route with loot-box parts.

entries.name: Roland / The Black Silence
entries.obtain: Start with Olivier in Dark Alley at Mastery 6 and Standless, then complete the Perception-Blocking Mask, Harold, AMM, and Library Invitation questline.
entries.best_for: Players who want a long route with boss weapon collection.

entries.name: The Red Mist
entries.obtain: Use Mimicry on Standless after defeating The Red Mist boss and completing the E.G.O. route.
entries.best_for: Players who enjoy hard PvE routes and lifesteal pressure.

entries.name: Kurosaki Ichigo
entries.obtain: Progress through Hollow and Soul Reaper routes, then use Urahara Kisuke's questline and Hogyoku Fragment for later forms.
entries.best_for: Players who want a multi-form PvE grind.

groups.summary: Specs unlocked through fruits, seasonal items, banners, events, or special shop routes.

entries.name: Mochi Mochi No Mi
entries.obtain: Use Mochi Mochi Devil Fruit on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players farming rare fruit-style unlocks.

entries.name: Bari Bari No Mi
entries.obtain: Use Bari Bari Devil Fruit on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players who want utility from rare item routes.

entries.name: Hie Hie No Mi
entries.obtain: Use Hie Hie Devil Fruit on Standless.
entries.best_for: Players who want freeze and control pressure.

entries.name: Padoru
entries.obtain: Use Bag of Presents on Standless.
entries.best_for: Collectors and seasonal-content players.

sakuraStandItemsFarming.title: Sakura Stand Items and Farming
sakuraStandItemsFarming.subtitle: Track the items that unlock Stands, Specs, questlines, bosses, and late-game routes in Sakura Stand.
sakuraStandItemsFarming.intro: Item farming in Sakura Stand connects directly to ability unlocks, quest routes, boss progression, and late-game upgrades. Arrows, Rokakaka, manuals, masks, boss drops, cyberware parts, and update items all have specific progression uses.

items.source: Boxes, chests, and common item farming routes
items.use: Use on Standless to roll Arrow-pool Stands such as Star Platinum, Crazy Diamond, Golden Experience, White Snake, and Emperor.
Resets the current Stand or Spec back to Standless.

items.source: Boxes, Auddy's Shop, and Arcade rolls
items.use: Use on Standless to obtain Hamon.
Use on Standless to obtain Vampirism.

items.source: Boxes, Bag of Presents, and item farming routes
items.use: Use on Standless to obtain Anubis.

items.source: Turtle Polnareff quest route and rare item sources
items.use: Use on Golden Experience to obtain Gold Experience Requiem.

items.source: Ability progression and item farming routes
items.use: Use on White Snake to obtain C-Moon.
Bring with C-Moon to the Highest Peak during a real-life New Moon for Made in Heaven progression.
Cyan Cyberdeck, Crimson Cyberdeck, Cortex Support, and Tech Monitor are required for the Sandevistan route.

items.source: Cursed spirit farming and player drops tied to vessel progress
items.use: Eat 15 with Itadori Yuji to unlock Sukuna's boss route.
Use on Standless to obtain Choso.
Use after reaching the required One For All stage and quest progress to obtain Deku.
Used in later Kurosaki Ichigo and Aizen-related progression.
Use on Standless to obtain The Red Mist.

sakuraStandBossesQuests.title: Sakura Stand Bosses, NPCs, and Quests
sakuraStandBossesQuests.subtitle: Use these route cards to jump from a target ability to the NPC, boss, location, and reward chain behind it in Sakura Stand.
sakuraStandBossesQuests.intro: Many Sakura Stand abilities are locked behind NPCs, boss drops, spawn timers, mastery gates, and multi-step routes. This module turns those routes into short front-page cards so players can find the right NPC or boss quickly.

routes.title: Naoya Zen'in Route
routes.npc: Naobito, John Zen'in, Naoya raid route
routes.location: Abandoned shrine and Naoya update areas
requirements: Do not equip a JJK Spec when starting the route
Talk to Naobito at the abandoned shrine
routes.goal: Farm the current Naoya update content and unlock a fast technical rushdown Spec.

routes.title: Choso Route
routes.goal: Unlock a blood manipulation Spec with combo pressure and awakening tools.

routes.title: Sukuna Route
routes.location: Side park and Sukuna boss arena
routes.goal: Convert Itadori Yuji into Sukuna through finger farming and boss completion.

routes.title: Toji Fushiguro Route
requirements: Complete the aura and item route
routes.goal: Farm boss weapon drops for a fast melee assassin route.

routes.title: Deku and One For All Route
routes.location: Kuzma spawn locations, Ruined City, forest tent area
requirements: Start One For All Stage 1 through Kuzma
Defeat Deku for One For All's Grace
routes.goal: Build from One For All into Deku through staged progression and repeated boss work.

routes.title: Roland / The Black Silence Route
routes.npc: Olivier, Harold, AMM, The Black Silence boss
routes.location: Dark Alley, forest camp, beach-side route, Library
requirements: Talk to Olivier in Dark Alley
Clear Harold's and AMM's tasks and the Library route
routes.goal: Follow one of the longest quest chains for a weapon-heavy combat Spec.

routes.title: The Red Mist Route
routes.npc: Carmen, Q3, The Red Mist boss
routes.location: Dark Alley and Red Mist portal route
requirements: Build progress by killing map enemies
Talk to Q3 to access the Red Mist encounter
routes.goal: Farm a hard boss route for a lifesteal-heavy E.G.O. ability.

routes.title: Sandevistan Route
requirements: Collect Cyan Cyberdeck, Crimson Cyberdeck, Cortex Support, and Tech Monitor
Use Arasaka Suitcase for Cyberskeleton progression
routes.goal: Turn cyberware loot-box parts into a speed-focused Spec route.

sakuraStandUpdates.title: Sakura Stand Updates and Patch Notes
sakuraStandUpdates.subtitle: Follow the latest Sakura Stand content changes, including new Specs, skins, codes, boss routes, bug fixes, and Trello updates.
sakuraStandUpdates.intro: The Roblox game page, official Discord, Sakura Society group, and official Trello are the main update path for Sakura Stand. This timeline is designed for ongoing homepage updates, so new Specs, codes, events, and balance changes can be added over time.

entries.label: Roblox Listing
entries.headline: Naoya and Choso update
changes: The Roblox experience title currently highlights Naoya and Choso.
Naoya Zen'in and Choso are the headline new Spec routes.
The update adds new code activity, new skins, and bug fixes.

entries.label: New Spec Route
entries.headline: Naoya Zen'in route added
changes: Naoya progression starts through the Zen'in route tied to Naobito and the abandoned shrine.
Naoya uses a fast technical combat style with multiple G variations.
Naoya is tied to the newer Domain Expansion rework direction.

entries.label: New Spec Route
entries.headline: Choso route added
changes: Choso is unlocked through Death Painting on Standless.
Death Painting is tied to the Kamo Remnant route.
Choso focuses on blood manipulation, stun pressure, and awakening tools.

entries.label: New Area and Farming
entries.headline: Kamo's Hideout and Kamo Remnant route
changes: Kamo's Hideout supports the Choso progression route.
Kamo Remnant farming connects to Death Painting.
John Zen'in appears as part of the Naoya and Choso update encounter pool.

entries.label: New Titles
entries.headline: Zen'in and blood-themed title goals
changes: Head of Zen'in Clan is tied to the Naoya update theme.
Zen'in Clan Member supports the new Zen'in progression path.
Blood Manipulator supports Choso progression and collection goals.

entries.label: Balance and Polish
entries.headline: Ability polish and bug-fix pass
changes: Several abilities received range, damage, and visual effect improvements.
Itadori Yuji received additional voice quote updates.
Multiple pose, dash, softlock, and interruption bugs were fixed across several abilities.

entries.label: Official Channels
entries.headline: Discord, Trello, and Roblox update tracking
changes: Developer announcements and patch note posts on Discord
Stand, Spec, item, and boss entries synced on Trello
New codes, skins, and event notices across official channels

faq.title: Sakura Stand
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about Sakura Stand codes, Stands, Specs, and progress

questions.question: What is Sakura Stand?
questions.answer: Sakura Stand is a Roblox anime fighting and farming experience developed by Sakura Society. It is inspired mainly by JoJo's Bizarre Adventure and other anime, with Stands and Specs as the core combat builds.

questions.question: How do I redeem Sakura Stand codes?
questions.answer: Open Sakura Stand on Roblox, press M or the menu button to open Settings, paste a working code into the Enter Code box, and press Enter. Codes can fail when expired, mistyped, mastery-locked, or already redeemed.

questions.question: What is the difference between Stands and Specs in Sakura Stand?
questions.answer: Stands usually come from Stand Arrows and Stand evolution items, while Specs come from manuals, masks, bosses, NPC quests, events, or special progression chains. Both are core combat identity choices in Sakura Stand.

questions.question: Where can I play Sakura Stand?
questions.answer: Sakura Stand is available on Roblox through the official game page by Sakura Society. The game supports PC and mobile players.

questions.question: Does Sakura Stand cost money?
questions.answer: Sakura Stand is free to play on Roblox. Optional purchases and Roblox Premium membership exist, and Premium grants a 1.15x XP gain bonus.

questions.question: Where can I find Sakura Stand update notes and new codes?
questions.answer: The official Discord, Trello, and Roblox game page are the main update sources for Sakura Stand. New Specs, skins, codes, and patch notes are posted there after each update.
